Title: Phylogenomics and metabolic engineering reveal a conserved gene cluster in Solanaceae plants for withanolide biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Withania somnifera; Physalis pruinosa; Physalis peruviana
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Withania somnifera; Physalis pruinosa; Physalis peruviana
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Withania somnifera; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: W. somnifera XX-0-STGAL—22/1985; N. benthamiana LAB; S. cerevisiae ST7574-derived strains
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: leaves (withaferin A measured in W. somnifera); Solanaceae medicinal tissues not fully specified
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: pharmaceutical/bioactive specialized metabolites with anti-stress and broad medicinal potential
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; virus-induced gene silencing in Withania somnifera; heterologous expression in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Committed-entry gene for redirecting phytosterol flux into withanolide biosynthesis; key chassis-engineering target for plant and microbial production platforms
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: converts 24-methylenecholesterol to 24-methyldesmosterol and enables entry into withanolide biosynthesis; silencing reduces withaferin A and causes accumulation of 24-methylenecholesterol
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: withanolide biosynthesis branching from phytosterol pathway
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: misbalance with 7RED in yeast causes shunt product ergosta-5,7,24-trien-3β-ol; pathway engineering burden
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 24ISO is the previously known committed-step enzyme that branches withanolide biosynthesis from general phytosterol metabolism. 2. Expression of 24ISO with upstream sterol-pathway engineering enabled 24-methyldesmosterol production in yeast and N. benthamiana. 3. VIGS of 24ISO in W. somnifera decreased withaferin A and caused accumulation of 24-methylenecholesterol.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: entry step from phytosterol pathway to withanolide branch
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: galactose-inducible promoters in yeast for some constructs; transient Agrobacterium expression vectors in N. benthamiana; specific promoter names mostly NA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: cluster-associated; two nearby copies in withanolide-producing species, with subcluster-specific expression patterns
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: silenced/tested in W. somnifera leaves; transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Only previously characterized withanolide-specific enzyme before this study; two clustered copies in producers and a third extra copy in W. somnifera
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: isomerizes the side-chain Δ24(28) double bond of 24-methylenecholesterol to form 24-methyldesmosterol
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: genome sequencing ENA ERP150021 / BioProject PRJEB64854; RNA-seq ENA ERR13615536; public RNA-seq datasets analyzed for cluster expression

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Withania somnifera; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: W. somnifera XX-0-STGAL—22/1985; N. benthamiana LAB; engineered yeast strains
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: leaves in W. somnifera for withaferin A assay
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: medicinal specialized metabolites with pharmaceutical potential
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: heterologous pathway reconstitution in N. benthamiana and yeast; VIGS in W. somnifera
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential early oxidation gene for reconstructing withanolide core pathway; suitable for pathway stacking with 24ISO, CYP88C7, CYP749B2 and SDR
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: hydroxylates 24-methyldesmosterol at C-22 to produce intermediate 10; silencing reduces withaferin A and causes accumulation of 24-methyldesmosterol
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Arabidopsis thaliana cytochrome P450 reductase used in yeast support system
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: host-dependent shunt products observed in yeast and N. benthamiana due to background metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP87G1 homologues from both W. somnifera subclusters and P. pruinosa catalyze the same first oxidation after 24ISO. 2. The enzyme converts 24-methyldesmosterol to (22R)-ergosta-5,24-diene-3β,22-diol in both yeast and N. benthamiana. 3. VIGS in W. somnifera caused reduced withaferin A and accumulation of 24-methyldesmosterol, validating its in planta role.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: oxidative assembly of side-chain lactone, first dedicated oxidation
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: transient Agrobacterium expression vectors in N. benthamiana; yeast expression constructs, specific promoters NA
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: cytochrome P450 monooxygenase, CYP87 family, CYP85 clan
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: cluster-associated; conserved in both sub gene clusters with differing subcluster expression patterns
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: tested in N. benthamiana leaves and W. somnifera leaves
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Assigned trivial name W22H; function conserved between species and between subclusters
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: hydroxylates 24-methyldesmosterol at carbon 22 to form the 22R alcohol intermediate
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: genome sequencing ENA ERP150021 / BioProject PRJEB64854; RNA-seq ENA ERR13615536 plus public RNA-seq datasets

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Withania somnifera; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: W. somnifera XX-0-STGAL—22/1985; N. benthamiana LAB; engineered yeast strains
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: leaves in W. somnifera for withaferin A assay
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: heterologous co-expression with CYP87G1 in N. benthamiana and yeast; VIGS in W. somnifera
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Required mid-pathway oxidation gene for assembling withanolide scaffold; useful in combinatorial biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: hydroxylates CYP87G1 product at C-1 to yield intermediate 14; silencing reduces withaferin A and causes accumulation of intermediate 10
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Arabidopsis thaliana cytochrome P450 reductase used in yeast support system
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: host-specific shunt products in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP88C7 acts after CYP87G1 and introduces the C-1 hydroxyl group. 2. Activity is conserved between W. somnifera subclusters and P. pruinosa. 3. VIGS caused a strong drop in withaferin A and accumulation of the CYP87G1 product, supporting in planta function.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: oxidative assembly of side-chain lactone and A-ring preparation
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: transient Agrobacterium expression vectors in N. benthamiana; yeast expression constructs, specific promoters NA
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: cytochrome P450 monooxygenase, CYP88 family, CYP85 clan
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: tested in N. benthamiana leaves and W. somnifera leaves
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Assigned trivial name W1H; lactone formation did not occur before this C-1 hydroxylation step
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: hydroxylates the CYP87G1-generated 22-hydroxysterol at carbon 1, likely giving the 1α-hydroxy intermediate
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: genome sequencing ENA ERP150021 / BioProject PRJEB64854; RNA-seq ENA ERR13615536 plus public RNA-seq datasets

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: W. somnifera XX-0-STGAL—22/1985; N. benthamiana LAB
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: leaves in W. somnifera for withaferin A assay
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: heterologous co-expression in N. benthamiana and yeast; in vitro assay with microsomes; VIGS in W. somnifera
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Critical oxidation gene for δ-lactone ring construction; must be stacked with SDR for efficient aglycone formation especially in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: oxidizes C-26 of intermediate 14 toward lactone formation; with SDR enables withanoside V aglycone production; silencing reduces withaferin A and causes accumulation of intermediate 14
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: (22R)-ergosta-5,24-diene-1α,3β,22,26-tetrol and downstream oxidized intermediates leading to withanoside V aglycone
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target-product side-chain oxidation state
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: alone insufficient for full lactone formation in yeast; one W. somnifera homolog from one subcluster lacked detectable activity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP749B2 is the dedicated C-26 oxidase acting after CYP87G1 and CYP88C7. 2. Together with SDR it supports formation of withanoside V aglycone; in yeast without SDR it accumulates tetrol 17 rather than the lactone. 3. VIGS in W. somnifera caused reduced withaferin A and accumulation of intermediate 14.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: transient Agrobacterium expression vectors in N. benthamiana; yeast expression constructs, specific promoters NA
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: cytochrome P450 monooxygenase, CYP749 family, CYP72 clan
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: tested in N. benthamiana leaves and W. somnifera leaves
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Assigned trivial name W26O; catalysis likely includes alcohol-to-aldehyde/lactol progression but requires SDR assistance for robust lactone completion
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: oxidatively modifies carbon 26 of the triol intermediate, initiating/advancing side-chain lactone formation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: genome sequencing ENA ERP150021 / BioProject PRJEB64854; RNA-seq ENA ERR13615536 plus public RNA-seq datasets

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Ashwagandha; Nicotiana benthamiana; yeast; Escherichia coli in vitro protein production
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Withania somnifera; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: W. somnifera XX-0-STGAL—22/1985; N. benthamiana LAB
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: leaves in W. somnifera for withaferin A assay
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: heterologous co-expression in N. benthamiana and yeast; in vitro assays with purified proteins; VIGS in W. somnifera
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential auxiliary oxidation enzyme for efficient lactone closure in microbial systems; boosts aglycone output in plant chassis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: converts late C-26 oxidation intermediates to the δ-lactone withanoside V aglycone and increases product formation 3–7 fold in N. benthamiana; necessary in yeast for lactone formation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: putative aldehyde/lactol intermediates derived from tetrol 17; can also weakly oxidize alcohol 17
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: silencing did not strongly reduce withaferin A in W. somnifera, suggesting endogenous background enzymes can partially compensate in planta
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SDR homologues complete/boost lactone formation after CYP749B2-dependent oxidation. 2. In yeast, SDR is required to obtain withanoside V aglycone, while in N. benthamiana it increases aglycone accumulation several-fold. 3. In vitro, SDR catalyzes the final oxidation to the lactone and can weakly perform the preceding alcohol oxidation step as well.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: terminal determinant in heterologous yeast system
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: transient Agrobacterium expression vectors in N. benthamiana; yeast expression constructs, specific promoters NA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: tested in N. benthamiana leaves and W. somnifera leaves
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Assigned trivial name W26DH; redundancy/background activity in planta likely masks VIGS phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: oxidizes late C-26 alcohol/aldehyde or lactol intermediates to the δ-lactone product withanoside V aglycone
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: genome sequencing ENA ERP150021 / BioProject PRJEB64854; RNA-seq ENA ERR13615536 plus public RNA-seq datasets
